MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-22-2018 IN 206, DEFENDANT HOSPITALITY INVESTMENT GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. DUE TO AN EMERGENCY, JUDGE JACKSON WAS NOT PRESENT BUT ISSUED HER TENTATIVE RULING THROUGH THE CALENDAR COORDINATOR. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendants Kimpton Group Holding, Llc, Kimpton Hotel And Restaurant Group, Llc, Khp Capital Partners, L.P. And Hospitality Investment Group, Inc.
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sara M. Peters, Esq. of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger, 415.981.7210, appearing for plaintiff;
Nandor Krause, Esq. of Archer Norris, 925.930.6600, appearing for defendants.

Ruling: ARGUED. The court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Kimpton Group Holding, LLC, Kimpton Hotel & Restaurant Group, LLC, KHP Capital Partners, L.P. and Hospitality Investment Group, Inc. (calling themselves the "Kimpton entities") move for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. The case involves plaintiff's fall down a hotel staircase allegedly due to lack of a handrail required by building codes. The Kimpton entities sold the hotel in 2010, but continued to manage it until a month before the fall.
The notice of motion fails to state its grounds, merely reciting the statutory summary judgment standard. From what I glean in the Kimpton entities' memorandum, they have essentially one argument: that "former owners/operators of property cease to be liable for dangerous conditions causing injury at the property upon the relinquishment of ownership and control, except under specified exceptions" set out in Preston v. Goldman (1986) 42 Cal.3d 108. Plaintiff opposes the motion by raising mixed issues of fact and law, at least two of which are well-taken. First, plaintiff argues that Preston does not apply to property which is opened to the general public, as the hotel staircase allegedly was. (See 42 Cal.3d at 116.) The Kimpton entities reply with Leakes v. Shamoun (1986) 187 Cal.App.3d 771, but plaintiff in that case was injured outside the property at issue. (Id.) At a minimum, material disputes exist over how open the hotel staircase was to the general public. (See, e.g., RPSS 34-36.) Second, one of Preston's exceptions is for "commercial builders, contractors or renovators," and the Kimpton entities are alleged to have renovated the hotel. (See 42 Cal. 3d n.10; RPSS 1-18.) The Kimpton entities argue the renovation's scope and that they are not professional renovators. Again, these are disputed issues of fact.
The Kimpton entities' motion is DENIED in its entirety. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, Tel. 415.551.5868, Emails: torreanom@aol.com/mtoreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
